item.title: BOK Holds Rates Steady Amid Uncertain Outlook for Policy
item.description: South Korea’s central bank kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged Thursday, maintaining a cautious stance as policymakers weighed rising inflation pressure from higher oil prices against uncertainty surrounding the broader economic outlook.

item.title: AI Boom Fuels Record $14.5 Billion in Taiwan Tech Firm Borrowing
item.description: Taiwanese tech firms have completed a record $14.5 billion of debt deals so far this year, as they race to secure financing to meet soaring demand for artificial intelligence capacity.

item.title: Japan Intervention Data Eyed as Yen Hovers Near 160 Per Dollar
item.description: Currency traders are counting down to Friday for official data from Japan’s finance ministry that will show how aggressively authorities intervened to support the yen over the past month.

item.title: World’s Appetite for AI Makes China Less Afraid of Stronger Yuan
item.description: The global AI investment boom is powering a new wave of Chinese exports and making Beijing more comfortable with a stronger yuan.

item.title: Swiggy CEO Vows to Stay Out of Amazon-Walmart India Spending War
item.description: Swiggy Ltd., one of India’s top quick commerce firms, plans to restrain spending and focus on profitability while deeper-pocketed rivals Flipkart and Amazon.com Inc. intensify efforts to shrink delivery times and expand discounts.

item.title: Fed’s Jefferson Warns of Inflation Risks From Energy Price Surge
item.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson said he expects inflation to cool later this year as the effects of tariffs and higher energy costs wear off, though he warned inflationary risks remain tilted to the upside.

item.title: Takaichi to Meet Philippines’ Marcos to Deepen Security Ties
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. is set to meet Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi on Thursday in Tokyo, as shared concerns over an increasingly assertive China accelerate a deepening of security ties between the two Asian nations.

item.title: War Stress Pushes Indonesia, Thailand to Lean on Short-Term Debt
item.description: Southeast Asia’s two biggest economies are selling more short-term debt to cope with the stress from the US-Iran war, draining liquidity from broader markets.

item.title: Gold Holds Losses as US-Iran Impasse Keeps Inflation Risk High
item.description: Gold held a two-day drop, with a lack of progress in US-Iran peace talks continuing to pressure interest rates.

item.title: UK Food Firms Plan to Raise Their Prices as Iran War Takes Toll
item.description: British food and drink manufacturers plan to raise prices in response to higher costs caused by the Middle East conflict, a move that threatens to pile more pressure on households.

item.title: Hong Kong to Get Edge in Asia Gold-Hub Push With Clearing System
item.description: By launching a gold-clearing system in the next couple of months, Hong Kong is set to secure first-mover advantage in a push to become Asia’s preeminent hub for bullion trading.

item.title: Synopsys Settles with Elliott, Names Firm’s Jesse Cohn to Board
item.description: Synopsys Inc. said it has reached an agreement with Elliott Investment Management and is appointing the activist investor’s Jesse Cohn to the chip-design software maker’s board.

item.title: Stocks in Asia to Slip on Mixed US-Iran Signals: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were set to edge lower, while oil gained as investors weighed conflicting signals over prospects for a deal to end the war in Iran and restore energy flows through the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Ohio to Halt Data Center Tax Credits as Opposition Grows
item.description: Ohio will pause incentives for future data center projects, following a directive from the governor to halt the tax credits while a committee studies the impact of the projects on the state’s economy.

item.title: Oil Steadies After Slump as Deal to End Iran War Remains Elusive
item.description: Oil steadied after slumping more as 5% on Wednesday, as the US and Iran remained at odds over how to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UK Faces ‘Lost Generation’ From Youth Unemployment, Review Says
item.description: A surge in UK youth unemployment risks creating a “lost generation,” according to a government-commissioned review that said urgent action is needed to correct a “whole-system failure.”

item.title: Dell Wins $9.7 Billion Defense Deal to Streamline Software Buys
item.description: A unit of Dell Technologies Inc. won a $9.7 billion contract to help the US military handle its licenses for Microsoft Corp. software related to email, spreadsheets and other needs across classified and unclassified systems, the Pentagon said.

item.title: Google Engineer Charged With Insider Trading on Polymarket
item.description: A Google software engineer was charged with insider trading on Polymarket, where he allegedly made more than $1 million betting on one of last year’s most popular Internet searches.

item.title: KKR’s Nuttall Says Trading Private Credit Is ‘Likely to Happen’
item.description: KKR & Co.’s co-chief executive officer Scott Nuttall said it was likely that the alternative asset manager would eventually start trading private credit, following early efforts by Apollo Global Management Inc. to create a market for less liquid debt.

item.title: Marvell Boosts Annual Forecast, Citing AI-Fueled Demand
item.description: Marvell Technology Inc. delivered a quarterly forecast that exceeded analysts’ estimates and boosted its outlook for the year, citing demand for chips used in AI data centers.

item.title: US Bonds’ Return to Pre-War Calm Fuels Bets It’ll Be Short-Lived
item.description: The return of calm to the US bond market following turbulence stirred up by the Iran war is attracting big moves in options, with some traders wagering it won’t last.

item.title: HP Gives Profit Outlook That Tops Estimate, Managing Chip Crunch
item.description: HP Inc. gave a profit forecast for the current quarter that topped analysts’ estimates, signaling that the company is weathering a dramatic increase in memory chip prices. The shares jumped in extended trading.

item.title: Snowflake Jumps 30% on Sales Outlook, $6 Billion Amazon Deal
item.description: Snowflake Inc. shares jumped almost 30% in late trading after the software maker gave a stronger-than-expected annual outlook and signed a $6 billion multiyear agreement to use Amazon.com Inc.’s cloud services and chips.

item.title: Fuel-Starved Cuba Dealt Blow as Russian Tanker Changes Course
item.description: A Russian tanker carrying over 240,000 barrels of diesel has abandoned its voyage to Cuba and is now sailing southeast after weeks of uncertainty surrounding a shipment meant to help relieve the fuel-starved island’s worsening energy crisis.

item.title: Salesforce Gives Lukewarm Outlook That Fuels Disruption Fear
item.description: Salesforce Inc. gave a revenue outlook for the current period that just fell short of analysts’ estimates, unnerving investors already concerned about the possibility that artificial intelligence will disrupt the software business.

item.title: Fed’s Cook Prepared to Raise Rates If Inflation Lingers
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook said inflation is headed in the wrong direction and she would be prepared to raise interest rates if that persists.

item.title: Three Charts Capturing Micron’s Record Rise to $1 Trillion
item.description: Micron Technology Inc.’s incredible rally this year has launched the memory-chip firm into an elite group of stocks worth at least $1 trillion, setting at least one record along the way.

item.title: The SpaceX IPO Is the Perfect Embodiment of Markets
item.description: Momentum trumps everything.

item.title: Mexico Cuts 2026 Growth Forecast as Investment Falters
item.description: Mexico’s central bank cut its estimate for 2026 growth to 1.1% from a previous 1.6%, as investment disappoints amid trade uncertainty and supply-chain disruptions.

item.title: Valve Raises Steam Deck Pricing By Up to $300 Amid Chip Shortage
item.description: Valve Corp. increased the prices of its Steam Deck handheld gaming devices more than 40%, the latest example of an industrywide memory and storage chip crunch that has driven up prices of many consumer gadgets.
